parts.text: Single task: inspect existing tool files and return the canonical pattern for a new tool file in internal/tool (entry function structure, Definition shape, Execute function placement, verbose logging pattern). Include concrete file references and minimal code-shape bullets only. Thoroughness medium.

state.output:
<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/tool.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"io"
7: 	"strings"
8: 	"time"
9: 
10: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/agent"
11: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/config"
12: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/memory"
13: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
14: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/resolve"
15: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
16: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/xdg"
17: )
18: 
19: // CallAgentToolName is the constant name for the sub-agent invocation tool.
20: const CallAgentToolName = toolname.CallAgent
21: 
22: // maxConversationTurns is the safety limit for the conversation loop.
23: const maxConversationTurns = 50
24: 
25: // ExecuteOptions holds configuration for executing a call_agent tool call.
26: type ExecuteOptions struct {
27: 	AllowedAgents []string
28: 	ParentModel   string
29: 	Depth         int
30: 	MaxDepth      int
31: 	Timeout       int
32: 	GlobalConfig  *config.GlobalConfig
33: 	Verbose       bool
34: 	Stderr        io.Writer
35: }
36: 
37: // CallAgentTool returns the call_agent tool definition for LLM tool calling.
38: // The allowedAgents list is included in the description and parameter descriptions
39: // so the LLM knows which agent names are valid.
40: func CallAgentTool(allowedAgents []string) provider.Tool {
41: 	agentList := strings.Join(allowedAgents, ", ")
42: 
43: 	return provider.Tool{
44: 		Name:        CallAgentToolName,
45: 		Description: "Delegate a task to a sub-agent. The sub-agent runs independently with its own context and returns only its final result. Available agents: " + agentList,
46: 		Parameters: map[string]provider.ToolParameter{
47: 			"agent": {
48: 				Type:        "string",
49: 				Description: "Name of the sub-agent to invoke (must be one of: " + agentList + ")",
50: 				Required:    true,
51: 			},
52: 			"task": {
53: 				Type:        "string",
54: 				Description: "What you need the sub-agent to do",
55: 				Required:    true,
56: 			},
57: 			"context": {
58: 				Type:        "string",
59: 				Description: "Additional context from your conversation to pass along",
60: 				Required:    false,
61: 			},
62: 		},
63: 	}
64: }
65: 
66: // ExecuteCallAgent executes a call_agent tool call by loading and running a sub-agent.
67: // It always returns a ToolResult (never an error). Errors are communicated via
68: // ToolResult.Content and ToolResult.IsError fields.
69: func ExecuteCallAgent(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, opts ExecuteOptions) provider.ToolResult {
70: 	// Step 1: Extract arguments
71: 	agentName := call.Arguments["agent"]
72: 	task := call.Arguments["task"]
73: 	taskContext := call.Arguments["context"]
74: 
75: 	// Step 2: Validate agent name
76: 	if agentName == "" {
77: 		return provider.ToolResult{
78: 			CallID:  call.ID,
79: 			Content: `call_agent error: "agent" argument is required`,
80: 			IsError: true,
81: 		}
82: 	}
83: 
84: 	// Step 3: Validate task
85: 	if task == "" {
86: 		return provider.ToolResult{
87: 			CallID:  call.ID,
88: 			Content: `call_agent error: "task" argument is required`,
89: 			IsError: true,
90: 		}
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	// Step 4: Validate agent is allowed
94: 	allowed := false
95: 	for _, a := range opts.AllowedAgents {
96: 		if a == agentName {
97: 			allowed = true
98: 			break
99: 		}
100: 	}
101: 	if !allowed {
102: 		return provider.ToolResult{
103: 			CallID:  call.ID,
104: 			Content: fmt.Sprintf("call_agent error: agent %q is not in this agent's sub_agents list", agentName),
105: 			IsError: true,
106: 		}
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	// Step 5: Check depth limit
110: 	if opts.Depth >= opts.MaxDepth {
111: 		return provider.ToolResult{
112: 			CallID:  call.ID,
113: 			Content: fmt.Sprintf("call_agent error: maximum sub-agent depth (%d) reached", opts.MaxDepth),
114: 			IsError: true,
115: 		}
116: 	}
117: 
118: 	// Verbose: log before sub-agent call
119: 	if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
120: 		taskPreview := task
121: 		if len(taskPreview) > 80 {
122: 			taskPreview = taskPreview[:80] + "..."
123: 		}
124: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] Calling %q (depth %d) with task: %s\n", agentName, opts.Depth+1, taskPreview)
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	start := time.Now()
128: 
129: 	// Step 6: Load sub-agent config
130: 	cfg, err := agent.Load(agentName)
131: 	if err != nil {
132: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("failed to load agent %q: %s", agentName, err), opts)
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	// Step 7: Parse sub-agent's model
136: 	provName, modelName, err := parseModel(cfg.Model)
137: 	if err != nil {
138: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("invalid model for agent %q: %s", agentName, err), opts)
139: 	}
140: 
141: 	// Step 8: Resolve sub-agent's working directory, files, skill, system prompt
142: 	workdir, err := resolve.Workdir("", cfg.Workdir)
143: 	if err != nil {
144: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("failed to resolve workdir for agent %q: %s", agentName, err), opts)
145: 	}
146: 
147: 	files, err := resolve.Files(cfg.Files, workdir)
148: 	if err != nil {
149: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("failed to resolve files for agent %q: %s", agentName, err), opts)
150: 	}
151: 
152: 	configDir, err := xdg.GetConfigDir()
153: 	if err != nil {
154: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("failed to get config dir: %s", err), opts)
155: 	}
156: 
157: 	skillContent, err := resolve.Skill(cfg.Skill, configDir)
158: 	if err != nil {
159: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("failed to load skill for agent %q: %s", agentName, err), opts)
160: 	}
161: 
162: 	systemPrompt := resolve.BuildSystemPrompt(cfg.SystemPrompt, skillContent, files)
163: 
164: 	// Step 8b: Memory — load entries into system prompt
165: 	if cfg.Memory.Enabled {
166: 		memPath, memErr := memory.FilePath(agentName, cfg.Memory.Path)
167: 		if memErr != nil {
168: 			if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
169: 				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] Warning: failed to load memory for %q: %v\n", agentName, memErr)
170: 			}
171: 		} else {
172: 			entries, memErr := memory.LoadEntries(memPath, cfg.Memory.LastN)
173: 			if memErr != nil {
174: 				if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
175: 					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] Warning: failed to load memory for %q: %v\n", agentName, memErr)
176: 				}
177: 			} else if entries != "" {
178: 				systemPrompt += "\n\n---\n\n## Memory\n\n" + entries
179: 			}
180: 
181: 			memCount, memErr := memory.CountEntries(memPath)
182: 			if memErr != nil {
183: 				if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
184: 					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] Warning: failed to count memory entries for %q: %v\n", agentName, memErr)
185: 				}
186: 			} else if cfg.Memory.MaxEntries > 0 && memCount >= cfg.Memory.MaxEntries {
187: 				if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
188: 					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] Warning: agent %q memory has %d entries (max_entries: %d). Run 'axe gc %s' to trim.\n", agentName, memCount, cfg.Memory.MaxEntries, agentName)
189: 				}
190: 			}
191: 		}
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	// Step 9: Resolve API key and base URL
195: 	globalCfg := opts.GlobalConfig
196: 	if globalCfg == nil {
197: 		globalCfg = &config.GlobalConfig{}
198: 	}
199: 
200: 	apiKey := globalCfg.ResolveAPIKey(provName)
201: 	baseURL := globalCfg.ResolveBaseURL(provName)
202: 
203: 	if provider.Supported(provName) && provName != "ollama" && apiKey == "" {
204: 		envVar := config.APIKeyEnvVar(provName)
205: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("API key for provider %q is not configured (set %s or add to config.toml)", provName, envVar), opts)
206: 	}
207: 
208: 	// Step 10: Create provider
209: 	prov, err := provider.New(provName, apiKey, baseURL)
210: 	if err != nil {
211: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("failed to create provider for agent %q: %s", agentName, err), opts)
212: 	}
213: 
214: 	// Step 11: Build user message
215: 	var userMessage string
216: 	if strings.TrimSpace(taskContext) != "" {
217: 		userMessage = fmt.Sprintf("Task: %s\n\nContext:\n%s", task, taskContext)
218: 	} else {
219: 		userMessage = fmt.Sprintf("Task: %s", task)
220: 	}
221: 
222: 	// Step 12: Build request
223: 	req := &provider.Request{
224: 		Model:       modelName,
225: 		System:      systemPrompt,
226: 		Messages:    []provider.Message{{Role: "user", Content: userMessage}},
227: 		Temperature: cfg.Params.Temperature,
228: 		MaxTokens:   cfg.Params.MaxTokens,
229: 	}
230: 
231: 	// Inject tools if sub-agent has sub_agents and depth allows
232: 	newDepth := opts.Depth + 1
233: 	if len(cfg.SubAgents) > 0 && newDepth < opts.MaxDepth {
234: 		req.Tools = []provider.Tool{CallAgentTool(cfg.SubAgents)}
235: 	}
236: 
237: 	// Resolve configured tools for the sub-agent
238: 	registry := NewRegistry()
239: 	RegisterAll(registry)
240: 	if len(cfg.Tools) > 0 {
241: 		resolvedTools, resolveErr := registry.Resolve(cfg.Tools)
242: 		if resolveErr != nil {
243: 			return provider.ToolResult{
244: 				CallID:  call.ID,
245: 				Content: fmt.Sprintf("failed to resolve tools for agent %q: %s", agentName, resolveErr),
246: 				IsError: true,
247: 			}
248: 		}
249: 		req.Tools = append(req.Tools, resolvedTools...)
250: 	}
251: 
252: 	// Step 13: Create timeout context
253: 	var callCtx context.Context
254: 	var cancel context.CancelFunc
255: 	if opts.Timeout > 0 {
256: 		callCtx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Duration(opts.Timeout)*time.Second)
257: 	} else {
258: 		callCtx, cancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)
259: 	}
260: 	defer cancel()
261: 
262: 	// Step 14: Run conversation loop (or single-shot if no tools)
263: 	resp, err := runConversationLoop(callCtx, prov, req, cfg, registry, newDepth, opts, workdir)
264: 	if err != nil {
265: 		durationMs := time.Since(start).Milliseconds()
266: 		if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
267: 			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] %q failed: %s\n", agentName, err)
268: 		}
269: 		_ = durationMs
270: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, err.Error(), opts)
271: 	}
272: 
273: 	// Step 14b: Memory — append entry after successful response
274: 	if cfg.Memory.Enabled {
275: 		appendPath, appendErr := memory.FilePath(agentName, cfg.Memory.Path)
276: 		if appendErr != nil {
277: 			if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
278: 				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] Warning: failed to save memory for %q: %v\n", agentName, appendErr)
279: 			}
280: 		} else {
281: 			if appendErr = memory.AppendEntry(appendPath, userMessage, resp.Content); appendErr != nil {
282: 				if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
283: 					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] Warning: failed to save memory for %q: %v\n", agentName, appendErr)
284: 				}
285: 			}
286: 		}
287: 	}
288: 
289: 	// Step 15: Return result
290: 	durationMs := time.Since(start).Milliseconds()
291: 	if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
292: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] %q completed in %dms (%d chars returned)\n", agentName, durationMs, len(resp.Content))
293: 	}
294: 
295: 	return provider.ToolResult{
296: 		CallID:  call.ID,
297: 		Content: resp.Content,
298: 		IsError: false,
299: 	}
300: }
301: 
302: // runConversationLoop runs the multi-turn conversation loop for a sub-agent.
303: // If the sub-agent has no tools, this is a single-shot call.
304: func runConversationLoop(ctx context.Context, prov provider.Provider, req *provider.Request, cfg *agent.AgentConfig, registry *Registry, depth int, opts ExecuteOptions, toolWorkdir string) (*provider.Response, error) {
305: 	for turn := 0; turn < maxConversationTurns; turn++ {
306: 		resp, err := prov.Send(ctx, req)
307: 		if err != nil {
308: 			return nil, err
309: 		}
310: 
311: 		// No tool calls: we're done
312: 		if len(resp.ToolCalls) == 0 {
313: 			return resp, nil
314: 		}
315: 
316: 		// If no tools were sent, treat as done even if response has tool calls
317: 		if len(req.Tools) == 0 {
318: 			return resp, nil
319: 		}
320: 
321: 		// Append assistant message with tool calls
322: 		assistantMsg := provider.Message{
323: 			Role:      "assistant",
324: 			Content:   resp.Content,
325: 			ToolCalls: resp.ToolCalls,
326: 		}
327: 		req.Messages = append(req.Messages, assistantMsg)
328: 
329: 		// Execute tool calls and collect results
330: 		results := make([]provider.ToolResult, len(resp.ToolCalls))
331: 		for i, tc := range resp.ToolCalls {
332: 			if tc.Name == CallAgentToolName {
333: 				subOpts := ExecuteOptions{
334: 					AllowedAgents: cfg.SubAgents,
335: 					ParentModel:   cfg.Model,
336: 					Depth:         depth,
337: 					MaxDepth:      opts.MaxDepth,
338: 					Timeout:       opts.Timeout,
339: 					GlobalConfig:  opts.GlobalConfig,
340: 					Verbose:       opts.Verbose,
341: 					Stderr:        opts.Stderr,
342: 				}
343: 				results[i] = ExecuteCallAgent(ctx, tc, subOpts)
344: 			} else {
345: 				execCtx := ExecContext{
346: 					Workdir: toolWorkdir,
347: 					Stderr:  opts.Stderr,
348: 					Verbose: opts.Verbose,
349: 				}
350: 				result, dispatchErr := registry.Dispatch(ctx, tc, execCtx)
351: 				if dispatchErr != nil {
352: 					results[i] = provider.ToolResult{
353: 						CallID:  tc.ID,
354: 						Content: dispatchErr.Error(),
355: 						IsError: true,
356: 					}
357: 				} else {
358: 					results[i] = result
359: 				}
360: 			}
361: 		}
362: 
363: 		// Append tool result message
364: 		toolMsg := provider.Message{
365: 			Role:        "tool",
366: 			ToolResults: results,
367: 		}
368: 		req.Messages = append(req.Messages, toolMsg)
369: 	}
370: 
371: 	return nil, fmt.Errorf("sub-agent exceeded maximum conversation turns (%d)", maxConversationTurns)
372: }
373: 
374: // errorResult creates an error ToolResult for a sub-agent failure.
375: func errorResult(callID, agentName, errMsg string, opts ExecuteOptions) provider.ToolResult {
376: 	if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
377: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] %q failed: %s\n", agentName, errMsg)
378: 	}
379: 	return provider.ToolResult{
380: 		CallID:  callID,
381: 		Content: fmt.Sprintf("Error: sub-agent %q failed - %s. You may retry or proceed without this result.", agentName, errMsg),
382: 		IsError: true,
383: 	}
384: }
385: 
386: // parseModel splits a "provider/model-name" string into provider and model parts.
387: func parseModel(model string) (providerName, modelName string, err error) {
388: 	idx := strings.Index(model, "/")
389: 	if idx < 0 {
390: 		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("invalid model format %q: expected provider/model-name", model)
391: 	}
392: 
393: 	providerName = model[:idx]
394: 	modelName = model[idx+1:]
395: 
396: 	if providerName == "" {
397: 		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("invalid model format %q: empty provider", model)
398: 	}
399: 	if modelName == "" {
400: 		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("invalid model format %q: empty model name", model)
401: 	}
402: 
403: 	return providerName, modelName, nil
404: }

(End of file - total 404 lines)
</content>

<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 
6: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
7: )
8: 
9: // toolVerboseLog emits a single log line to ec.Stderr when ec.Verbose is true.
10: // Format: [tool] <toolName>: <summary> (success|error)
11: func toolVerboseLog(ec ExecContext, toolName string, result provider.ToolResult, summary string) {
12: 	if !ec.Verbose || ec.Stderr == nil {
13: 		return
14: 	}
15: 
16: 	status := "success"
17: 	if result.IsError {
18: 		status = "error"
19: 	}
20: 
21: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(ec.Stderr, "[tool] %s: %s (%s)\n", toolName, summary, status)
22: }

(End of file - total 22 lines)

state.output:
<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/registry.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"io"
7: 
8: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
9: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
10: )
11: 
12: // ExecContext holds the minimal context needed by generic tool executors.
13: type ExecContext struct {
14: 	Workdir string
15: 	Stderr  io.Writer
16: 	Verbose bool
17: }
18: 
19: // ToolEntry holds a tool's definition and executor functions.
20: type ToolEntry struct {
21: 	Definition func() provider.Tool
22: 	Execute    func(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) provider.ToolResult
23: }
24: 
25: // Registry maps tool names to their definitions and executors.
26: // Registration must happen at startup before any concurrent Dispatch calls.
27: // Concurrent Dispatch calls (read-only) are safe after registration is complete.
28: type Registry struct {
29: 	entries map[string]ToolEntry
30: }
31: 
32: // NewRegistry returns a new Registry with an empty, initialized entries map.
33: func NewRegistry() *Registry {
34: 	return &Registry{
35: 		entries: make(map[string]ToolEntry),
36: 	}
37: }
38: 
39: // Register adds a tool entry to the registry, keyed by name.
40: // If a tool with the same name already exists, it is silently replaced.
41: func (r *Registry) Register(name string, entry ToolEntry) {
42: 	r.entries[name] = entry
43: }
44: 
45: // Has returns true if a tool with the given name exists in the registry.
46: func (r *Registry) Has(name string) bool {
47: 	_, ok := r.entries[name]
48: 	return ok
49: }
50: 
51: // Resolve takes a list of tool names and returns their provider.Tool definitions.
52: // Each entry's Definition() is called on every Resolve invocation (not cached).
53: // Returns an error if any name is not found in the registry or has a nil Definition.
54: // Returns an empty non-nil slice for nil or empty input.
55: func (r *Registry) Resolve(names []string) ([]provider.Tool, error) {
56: 	if len(names) == 0 {
57: 		return []provider.Tool{}, nil
58: 	}
59: 
60: 	tools := make([]provider.Tool, 0, len(names))
61: 	for _, name := range names {
62: 		entry, ok := r.entries[name]
63: 		if !ok {
64: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown tool %q", name)
65: 		}
66: 		if entry.Definition == nil {
67: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("tool %q has no definition", name)
68: 		}
69: 		tools = append(tools, entry.Definition())
70: 	}
71: 	return tools, nil
72: }
73: 
74: // Dispatch looks up the tool by call.Name and executes it.
75: // Returns (ToolResult, nil) on success, or (zero ToolResult, error) for
76: // unknown tools or tools with nil executors.
77: func (r *Registry) Dispatch(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) (provider.ToolResult, error) {
78: 	entry, ok := r.entries[call.Name]
79: 	if !ok {
80: 		return provider.ToolResult{}, fmt.Errorf("unknown tool %q", call.Name)
81: 	}
82: 	if entry.Execute == nil {
83: 		return provider.ToolResult{}, fmt.Errorf("tool %q has no executor", call.Name)
84: 	}
85: 	return entry.Execute(ctx, call, ec), nil
86: }
87: 
88: // RegisterAll registers all implemented tool entries into the given registry.
89: // Both cmd/run.go and ExecuteCallAgent call this after NewRegistry().
90: // Future milestones add lines here — no call-site changes needed.
91: // Safe to call multiple times (Register silently replaces duplicates).
92: func RegisterAll(r *Registry) {
93: 	r.Register(toolname.ListDirectory, listDirectoryEntry())
94: 	r.Register(toolname.ReadFile, readFileEntry())
95: 	r.Register(toolname.WriteFile, writeFileEntry())
96: 	r.Register(toolname.EditFile, editFileEntry())
97: 	r.Register(toolname.RunCommand, runCommandEntry())
98: 	r.Register(toolname.URLFetch, urlFetchEntry())
99: }

(End of file - total 99 lines)
</content>

<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"os"
7: 	"strings"
8: 
9: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
10: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
11: )
12: 
13: // listDirectoryEntry returns the ToolEntry for the list_directory tool.
14: func listDirectoryEntry() ToolEntry {
15: 	return ToolEntry{
16: 		Definition: listDirectoryDefinition,
17: 		Execute:    listDirectoryExecute,
18: 	}
19: }
20: 
21: func listDirectoryDefinition() provider.Tool {
22: 	return provider.Tool{
23: 		Name:        toolname.ListDirectory,
24: 		Description: "List the contents of a directory relative to the working directory. Returns one entry per line; subdirectories are suffixed with /.",
25: 		Parameters: map[string]provider.ToolParameter{
26: 			"path": {
27: 				Type:        "string",
28: 				Description: "Relative path to the directory to list. Use \".\" to list the working directory root.",
29: 				Required:    true,
30: 			},
31: 		},
32: 	}
33: }
34: 
35: func listDirectoryExecute(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) (result provider.ToolResult) {
36: 	path := call.Arguments["path"]
37: 
38: 	defer func() {
39: 		var summary string
40: 		if result.IsError {
41: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("path %q: %s", path, result.Content)
42: 		} else {
43: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("path %q", path)
44: 		}
45: 		toolVerboseLog(ec, toolname.ListDirectory, result, summary)
46: 	}()
47: 
48: 	resolved, err := validatePath(ec.Workdir, path)
49: 	if err != nil {
50: 		return provider.ToolResult{
51: 			CallID:  call.ID,
52: 			Content: err.Error(),
53: 			IsError: true,
54: 		}
55: 	}
56: 
57: 	entries, err := os.ReadDir(resolved)
58: 	if err != nil {
59: 		return provider.ToolResult{
60: 			CallID:  call.ID,
61: 			Content: err.Error(),
62: 			IsError: true,
63: 		}
64: 	}
65: 
66: 	if len(entries) == 0 {
67: 		return provider.ToolResult{
68: 			CallID:  call.ID,
69: 			Content: "",
70: 			IsError: false,
71: 		}
72: 	}
73: 
74: 	var b strings.Builder
75: 	for i, entry := range entries {
76: 		if i > 0 {
77: 			b.WriteByte('\n')
78: 		}
79: 		b.WriteString(entry.Name())
80: 		if entry.IsDir() {
81: 			b.WriteByte('/')
82: 		}
83: 	}
84: 
85: 	return provider.ToolResult{
86: 		CallID:  call.ID,
87: 		Content: b.String(),
88: 		IsError: false,
89: 	}
90: }

(End of file - total 90 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"bytes"
5: 	"context"
6: 	"fmt"
7: 	"os"
8: 	"strconv"
9: 	"strings"
10: 
11: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
12: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
13: )
14: 
15: // readFileEntry returns the ToolEntry for the read_file tool.
16: func readFileEntry() ToolEntry {
17: 	return ToolEntry{
18: 		Definition: readFileDefinition,
19: 		Execute:    readFileExecute,
20: 	}
21: }
22: 
23: func readFileDefinition() provider.Tool {
24: 	return provider.Tool{
25: 		Name:        toolname.ReadFile,
26: 		Description: "Read the contents of a file relative to the working directory. Returns line-numbered output in the format 'N: content' for each line.",
27: 		Parameters: map[string]provider.ToolParameter{
28: 			"path": {
29: 				Type:        "string",
30: 				Description: "Relative path to the file to read.",
31: 				Required:    true,
32: 			},
33: 			"offset": {
34: 				Type:        "string",
35: 				Description: "1-indexed line number to start reading from. Defaults to 1.",
36: 				Required:    false,
37: 			},
38: 			"limit": {
39: 				Type:        "string",
40: 				Description: "Maximum number of lines to return. Defaults to 2000.",
41: 				Required:    false,
42: 			},
43: 		},
44: 	}
45: }
46: 
47: func readFileExecute(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) (result provider.ToolResult) {
48: 	path := call.Arguments["path"]
49: 
50: 	defer func() {
51: 		var summary string
52: 		if result.IsError {
53: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("path %q: %s", path, result.Content)
54: 		} else {
55: 			lineCount := 0
56: 			if result.Content != "" {
57: 				lineCount = strings.Count(result.Content, "\n") + 1
58: 			}
59: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("path %q (%d lines)", path, lineCount)
60: 		}
61: 		toolVerboseLog(ec, toolname.ReadFile, result, summary)
62: 	}()
63: 
64: 	resolved, err := validatePath(ec.Workdir, path)
65: 	if err != nil {
66: 		return provider.ToolResult{
67: 			CallID:  call.ID,
68: 			Content: err.Error(),
69: 			IsError: true,
70: 		}
71: 	}
72: 
73: 	info, err := os.Stat(resolved)
74: 	if err != nil {
75: 		return provider.ToolResult{
76: 			CallID:  call.ID,
77: 			Content: err.Error(),
78: 			IsError: true,
79: 		}
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	if info.IsDir() {
83: 		return provider.ToolResult{
84: 			CallID:  call.ID,
85: 			Content: "path is a directory, not a file",
86: 			IsError: true,
87: 		}
88: 	}
89: 
90: 	content, err := os.ReadFile(resolved)
91: 	if err != nil {
92: 		return provider.ToolResult{
93: 			CallID:  call.ID,
94: 			Content: err.Error(),
95: 			IsError: true,
96: 		}
97: 	}
98: 
99: 	// Binary detection: scan first 512 bytes for NUL byte.
100: 	peek := content
101: 	if len(peek) > 512 {
102: 		peek = peek[:512]
103: 	}
104: 	if bytes.ContainsRune(peek, '\x00') {
105: 		return provider.ToolResult{
106: 			CallID:  call.ID,
107: 			Content: "binary file detected",
108: 			IsError: true,
109: 		}
110: 	}
111: 
112: 	// Parse offset (default 1).
113: 	offset := 1
114: 	if v := call.Arguments["offset"]; v != "" {
115: 		offset, err = strconv.Atoi(v)
116: 		if err != nil {
117: 			return provider.ToolResult{
118: 				CallID:  call.ID,
119: 				Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid offset %q: %s", v, err.Error()),
120: 				IsError: true,
121: 			}
122: 		}
123: 		if offset < 1 {
124: 			return provider.ToolResult{
125: 				CallID:  call.ID,
126: 				Content: "offset must be >= 1",
127: 				IsError: true,
128: 			}
129: 		}
130: 	}
131: 
132: 	// Parse limit (default 2000).
133: 	limit := 2000
134: 	if v := call.Arguments["limit"]; v != "" {
135: 		limit, err = strconv.Atoi(v)
136: 		if err != nil {
137: 			return provider.ToolResult{
138: 				CallID:  call.ID,
139: 				Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid limit %q: %s", v, err.Error()),
140: 				IsError: true,
141: 			}
142: 		}
143: 		if limit < 1 {
144: 			return provider.ToolResult{
145: 				CallID:  call.ID,
146: 				Content: "limit must be >= 1",
147: 				IsError: true,
148: 			}
149: 		}
150: 	}
151: 
152: 	// Empty file early return.
153: 	if len(content) == 0 {
154: 		if offset > 1 {
155: 			return provider.ToolResult{
156: 				CallID:  call.ID,
157: 				Content: fmt.Sprintf("offset %d exceeds file length of 0 lines", offset),
158: 				IsError: true,
159: 			}
160: 		}
161: 		return provider.ToolResult{
162: 			CallID:  call.ID,
163: 			Content: "",
164: 			IsError: false,
165: 		}
166: 	}
167: 
168: 	// Split into lines and handle trailing newline.
169: 	lines := strings.Split(string(content), "\n")
170: 	if len(content) > 0 && content[len(content)-1] == '\n' {
171: 		lines = lines[:len(lines)-1]
172: 	}
173: 
174: 	totalLines := len(lines)
175: 
176: 	// Offset bounds check.
177: 	if offset > totalLines {
178: 		return provider.ToolResult{
179: 			CallID:  call.ID,
180: 			Content: fmt.Sprintf("offset %d exceeds file length of %d lines", offset, totalLines),
181: 			IsError: true,
182: 		}
183: 	}
184: 
185: 	// Line selection.
186: 	start := offset - 1
187: 	end := start + limit
188: 	if end > totalLines {
189: 		end = totalLines
190: 	}
191: 	selected := lines[start:end]
192: 
193: 	// Output formatting.
194: 	var b strings.Builder
195: 	for i, line := range selected {
196: 		if i > 0 {
197: 			b.WriteByte('\n')
198: 		}
199: 		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "%d: %s", start+i+1, line)
200: 	}
201: 
202: 	return provider.ToolResult{
203: 		CallID:  call.ID,
204: 		Content: b.String(),
205: 		IsError: false,
206: 	}
207: }

(End of file - total 207 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"os"
7: 	"path/filepath"
8: 
9: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
10: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
11: )
12: 
13: // writeFileEntry returns the ToolEntry for the write_file tool.
14: func writeFileEntry() ToolEntry {
15: 	return ToolEntry{
16: 		Definition: writeFileDefinition,
17: 		Execute:    writeFileExecute,
18: 	}
19: }
20: 
21: func writeFileDefinition() provider.Tool {
22: 	return provider.Tool{
23: 		Name:        toolname.WriteFile,
24: 		Description: "Create or overwrite a file relative to the working directory. Creates parent directories as needed. Overwrites the file if it already exists.",
25: 		Parameters: map[string]provider.ToolParameter{
26: 			"path": {
27: 				Type:        "string",
28: 				Description: "Relative path to the file to write.",
29: 				Required:    true,
30: 			},
31: 			"content": {
32: 				Type:        "string",
33: 				Description: "The content to write to the file.",
34: 				Required:    false,
35: 			},
36: 		},
37: 	}
38: }
39: 
40: func writeFileExecute(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) (result provider.ToolResult) {
41: 	path := call.Arguments["path"]
42: 
43: 	defer func() {
44: 		var summary string
45: 		if result.IsError {
46: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("path %q: %s", path, result.Content)
47: 		} else {
48: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("path %q (%d bytes)", path, len(call.Arguments["content"]))
49: 		}
50: 		toolVerboseLog(ec, toolname.WriteFile, result, summary)
51: 	}()
52: 
53: 	// Empty path check.
54: 	if path == "" {
55: 		return provider.ToolResult{
56: 			CallID:  call.ID,
57: 			Content: "path is required",
58: 			IsError: true,
59: 		}
60: 	}
61: 
62: 	// Absolute path check.
63: 	if filepath.IsAbs(path) {
64: 		return provider.ToolResult{
65: 			CallID:  call.ID,
66: 			Content: "absolute paths are not allowed",
67: 			IsError: true,
68: 		}
69: 	}
70: 
71: 	// Traversal fast-path check.
72: 	cleanWorkdir := filepath.Clean(ec.Workdir)
73: 	resolved := filepath.Clean(filepath.Join(cleanWorkdir, path))
74: 
75: 	if !isWithinDir(resolved, cleanWorkdir) {
76: 		return provider.ToolResult{
77: 			CallID:  call.ID,
78: 			Content: "path escapes workdir",
79: 			IsError: true,
80: 		}
81: 	}
82: 
83: 	// Create parent directories.
84: 	parent := filepath.Dir(resolved)
85: 	if err := os.MkdirAll(parent, 0o755); err != nil {
86: 		return provider.ToolResult{
87: 			CallID:  call.ID,
88: 			Content: err.Error(),
89: 			IsError: true,
90: 		}
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	// Symlink escape check on parent directory.
94: 	evalParent, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(parent)
95: 	if err != nil {
96: 		return provider.ToolResult{
97: 			CallID:  call.ID,
98: 			Content: err.Error(),
99: 			IsError: true,
100: 		}
101: 	}
102: 
103: 	evalWorkdir, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(cleanWorkdir)
104: 	if err != nil {
105: 		return provider.ToolResult{
106: 			CallID:  call.ID,
107: 			Content: err.Error(),
108: 			IsError: true,
109: 		}
110: 	}
111: 
112: 	if !isWithinDir(evalParent, evalWorkdir) {
113: 		return provider.ToolResult{
114: 			CallID:  call.ID,
115: 			Content: "path escapes workdir",
116: 			IsError: true,
117: 		}
118: 	}
119: 
120: 	// Extract content (missing or empty key writes 0-byte file).
121: 	content := call.Arguments["content"]
122: 
123: 	// Write file.
124: 	data := []byte(content)
125: 	if err := os.WriteFile(resolved, data, 0o644); err != nil {
126: 		return provider.ToolResult{
127: 			CallID:  call.ID,
128: 			Content: err.Error(),
129: 			IsError: true,
130: 		}
131: 	}
132: 
133: 	return provider.ToolResult{
134: 		CallID:  call.ID,
135: 		Content: fmt.Sprintf("wrote %d bytes to %s", len(data), path),
136: 		IsError: false,
137: 	}
138: }

(End of file - total 138 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"os"
7: 	"strconv"
8: 	"strings"
9: 
10: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
11: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
12: )
13: 
14: // editFileEntry returns the ToolEntry for the edit_file tool.
15: func editFileEntry() ToolEntry {
16: 	return ToolEntry{
17: 		Definition: editFileDefinition,
18: 		Execute:    editFileExecute,
19: 	}
20: }
21: 
22: func editFileDefinition() provider.Tool {
23: 	return provider.Tool{
24: 		Name:        toolname.EditFile,
25: 		Description: "Find and replace exact text in an existing file relative to the working directory. Supports single replacement (default) and replace-all mode.",
26: 		Parameters: map[string]provider.ToolParameter{
27: 			"path": {
28: 				Type:        "string",
29: 				Description: "Relative path to the file to edit.",
30: 				Required:    true,
31: 			},
32: 			"old_string": {
33: 				Type:        "string",
34: 				Description: "The exact text to find in the file.",
35: 				Required:    true,
36: 			},
37: 			"new_string": {
38: 				Type:        "string",
39: 				Description: "The text to replace old_string with.",
40: 				Required:    true,
41: 			},
42: 			"replace_all": {
43: 				Type:        "string",
44: 				Description: "When set to \"true\", all occurrences are replaced. Defaults to false (single replacement).",
45: 				Required:    false,
46: 			},
47: 		},
48: 	}
49: }
50: 
51: func editFileExecute(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) (result provider.ToolResult) {
52: 	path := call.Arguments["path"]
53: 	replacedCount := 0
54: 
55: 	defer func() {
56: 		var summary string
57: 		if result.IsError {
58: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("path %q: %s", path, result.Content)
59: 		} else {
60: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("path %q (%d occurrence(s))", path, replacedCount)
61: 		}
62: 		toolVerboseLog(ec, toolname.EditFile, result, summary)
63: 	}()
64: 
65: 	// Path validation (handles empty, absolute, traversal, symlink escape, nonexistent).
66: 	resolved, err := validatePath(ec.Workdir, path)
67: 	if err != nil {
68: 		return provider.ToolResult{
69: 			CallID:  call.ID,
70: 			Content: err.Error(),
71: 			IsError: true,
72: 		}
73: 	}
74: 
75: 	// Directory rejection.
76: 	info, err := os.Stat(resolved)
77: 	if err != nil {
78: 		return provider.ToolResult{
79: 			CallID:  call.ID,
80: 			Content: err.Error(),
81: 			IsError: true,
82: 		}
83: 	}
84: 	if info.IsDir() {
85: 		return provider.ToolResult{
86: 			CallID:  call.ID,
87: 			Content: "path is a directory, not a file",
88: 			IsError: true,
89: 		}
90: 	}
91: 
92: 	// Extract old_string — empty is rejected.
93: 	oldString := call.Arguments["old_string"]
94: 	if oldString == "" {
95: 		return provider.ToolResult{
96: 			CallID:  call.ID,
97: 			Content: "old_string is required",
98: 			IsError: true,
99: 		}
100: 	}
101: 
102: 	// Extract new_string — empty is valid (deletion).
103: 	newString := call.Arguments["new_string"]
104: 
105: 	// Read file contents.
106: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(resolved)
107: 	if err != nil {
108: 		return provider.ToolResult{
109: 			CallID:  call.ID,
110: 			Content: err.Error(),
111: 			IsError: true,
112: 		}
113: 	}
114: 	content := string(data)
115: 
116: 	// Parse replace_all.
117: 	replaceAll := false
118: 	if v := call.Arguments["replace_all"]; v != "" {
119: 		replaceAll, err = strconv.ParseBool(v)
120: 		if err != nil {
121: 			return provider.ToolResult{
122: 				CallID:  call.ID,
123: 				Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid replace_all value %q: %s", v, err.Error()),
124: 				IsError: true,
125: 			}
126: 		}
127: 	}
128: 
129: 	// Count occurrences.
130: 	count := strings.Count(content, oldString)
131: 
132: 	if count == 0 {
133: 		return provider.ToolResult{
134: 			CallID:  call.ID,
135: 			Content: "old_string not found in file",
136: 			IsError: true,
137: 		}
138: 	}
139: 
140: 	if count > 1 && !replaceAll {
141: 		return provider.ToolResult{
142: 			CallID:  call.ID,
143: 			Content: fmt.Sprintf("old_string found %d times; set replace_all to true or provide a more unique string", count),
144: 			IsError: true,
145: 		}
146: 	}
147: 
148: 	// Perform replacement.
149: 	var newContent string
150: 	var replaced int
151: 	if replaceAll {
152: 		newContent = strings.ReplaceAll(content, oldString, newString)
153: 		replaced = count
154: 	} else {
155: 		newContent = strings.Replace(content, oldString, newString, 1)
156: 		replaced = 1
157: 	}
158: 
159: 	// Write file back.
160: 	if err := os.WriteFile(resolved, []byte(newContent), 0o644); err != nil {
161: 		return provider.ToolResult{
162: 			CallID:  call.ID,
163: 			Content: err.Error(),
164: 			IsError: true,
165: 		}
166: 	}
167: 
168: 	replacedCount = replaced
169: 
170: 	return provider.ToolResult{
171: 		CallID:  call.ID,
172: 		Content: fmt.Sprintf("replaced %d occurrence(s) in %s", replaced, path),
173: 		IsError: false,
174: 	}
175: }

(End of file - total 175 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"errors"
6: 	"fmt"
7: 	"os/exec"
8: 
9: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
10: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
11: )
12: 
13: // truncateCommand truncates a command string to maxLen chars, appending "..." if longer.
14: func truncateCommand(cmd string, maxLen int) string {
15: 	if len(cmd) <= maxLen {
16: 		return cmd
17: 	}
18: 	return cmd[:maxLen] + "..."
19: }
20: 
21: const maxOutputBytes = 100 * 1024 // 102400 bytes
22: 
23: // runCommandEntry returns the ToolEntry for the run_command tool.
24: func runCommandEntry() ToolEntry {
25: 	return ToolEntry{
26: 		Definition: runCommandDefinition,
27: 		Execute:    runCommandExecute,
28: 	}
29: }
30: 
31: func runCommandDefinition() provider.Tool {
32: 	return provider.Tool{
33: 		Name:        toolname.RunCommand,
34: 		Description: "Execute a shell command in the agent's working directory via sh -c and return the combined stdout/stderr output.",
35: 		Parameters: map[string]provider.ToolParameter{
36: 			"command": {
37: 				Type:        "string",
38: 				Description: "The shell command to execute.",
39: 				Required:    true,
40: 			},
41: 		},
42: 	}
43: }
44: 
45: func runCommandExecute(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) (result provider.ToolResult) {
46: 	command := call.Arguments["command"]
47: 	truncCmd := truncateCommand(command, 60)
48: 	var cmdErr error
49: 
50: 	defer func() {
51: 		var summary string
52: 		if result.IsError {
53: 			var exitErr *exec.ExitError
54: 			if errors.As(cmdErr, &exitErr) {
55: 				summary = fmt.Sprintf("%q (exit %d)", truncCmd, exitErr.ExitCode())
56: 			} else if cmdErr != nil {
57: 				summary = fmt.Sprintf("%q (%s)", truncCmd, cmdErr.Error())
58: 			} else {
59: 				summary = fmt.Sprintf("%q (%s)", truncCmd, result.Content)
60: 			}
61: 		} else {
62: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("%q (exit 0)", truncCmd)
63: 		}
64: 		toolVerboseLog(ec, toolname.RunCommand, result, summary)
65: 	}()
66: 
67: 	if command == "" {
68: 		return provider.ToolResult{
69: 			CallID:  call.ID,
70: 			Content: "command is required",
71: 			IsError: true,
72: 		}
73: 	}
74: 
75: 	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "sh", "-c", command)
76: 	cmd.Dir = ec.Workdir
77: 
78: 	output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
79: 
80: 	// Truncate output if it exceeds 100KB.
81: 	outStr := string(output)
82: 	if len(output) > maxOutputBytes {
83: 		outStr = string(output[:maxOutputBytes]) + "\n... [output truncated, exceeded 100KB]"
84: 	}
85: 
86: 	if err == nil {
87: 		return provider.ToolResult{
88: 			CallID:  call.ID,
89: 			Content: outStr,
90: 			IsError: false,
91: 		}
92: 	}
93: 
94: 	cmdErr = err
95: 
96: 	var exitErr *exec.ExitError
97: 	if errors.As(err, &exitErr) {
98: 		return provider.ToolResult{
99: 			CallID:  call.ID,
100: 			Content: fmt.Sprintf("exit code %d\n%s", exitErr.ExitCode(), outStr),
101: 			IsError: true,
102: 		}
103: 	}
104: 
105: 	return provider.ToolResult{
106: 		CallID:  call.ID,
107: 		Content: err.Error(),
108: 		IsError: true,
109: 	}
110: }

(End of file - total 110 lines)
</content>

state.output:
<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/url_fetch.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"io"
7: 	"net/http"
8: 	"net/url"
9: 
10: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
11: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
12: )
13: 
14: const maxReadBytes = 10000
15: 
16: func truncateURL(urlStr string, maxLen int) string {
17: 	if len(urlStr) <= maxLen {
18: 		return urlStr
19: 	}
20: 	return urlStr[:maxLen] + "..."
21: }
22: 
23: func sanitizeURL(urlStr string) string {
24: 	parsedURL, err := url.Parse(urlStr)
25: 	if err != nil {
26: 		return "invalid-url"
27: 	}
28: 
29: 	if parsedURL.Scheme == "" || parsedURL.Host == "" {
30: 		return "invalid-url"
31: 	}
32: 
33: 	path := parsedURL.EscapedPath()
34: 	if path == "" {
35: 		return parsedURL.Scheme + "://" + parsedURL.Host
36: 	}
37: 
38: 	return parsedURL.Scheme + "://" + parsedURL.Host + path
39: }
40: 
41: func urlFetchEntry() ToolEntry {
42: 	return ToolEntry{
43: 		Definition: urlFetchDefinition,
44: 		Execute:    urlFetchExecute,
45: 	}
46: }
47: 
48: func urlFetchDefinition() provider.Tool {
49: 	return provider.Tool{
50: 		Name:        toolname.URLFetch,
51: 		Description: "Fetch content from a URL using HTTP GET and return the response body as text.",
52: 		Parameters: map[string]provider.ToolParameter{
53: 			"url": {
54: 				Type:        "string",
55: 				Description: "The URL to fetch.",
56: 				Required:    true,
57: 			},
58: 		},
59: 	}
60: }
61: 
62: func urlFetchExecute(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) (result provider.ToolResult) {
63: 	urlStr := call.Arguments["url"]
64: 	statusCode := 0
65: 
66: 	defer func() {
67: 		safeURL := truncateURL(sanitizeURL(urlStr), 120)
68: 		summary := fmt.Sprintf("url %q", safeURL)
69: 		if statusCode != 0 {
70: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("url %q (HTTP %d)", safeURL, statusCode)
71: 		} else if result.IsError {
72: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", summary, truncateURL(result.Content, 120))
73: 		}
74: 		toolVerboseLog(ec, toolname.URLFetch, result, summary)
75: 	}()
76: 
77: 	if urlStr == "" {
78: 		return provider.ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, Content: "url is required", IsError: true}
79: 	}
80: 
81: 	parsedURL, err := url.Parse(urlStr)
82: 	if err != nil {
83: 		return provider.ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, Content: err.Error(), IsError: true}
84: 	}
85: 
86: 	if parsedURL.Scheme != "http" && parsedURL.Scheme != "https" {
87: 		return provider.ToolResult{
88: 			CallID:  call.ID,
89: 			Content: fmt.Sprintf("unsupported scheme %q: only http and https are allowed", parsedURL.Scheme),
90: 			IsError: true,
91: 		}
92: 	}
93: 
94: 	req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, urlStr, nil)
95: 	if err != nil {
96: 		return provider.ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, Content: err.Error(), IsError: true}
97: 	}
98: 
99: 	resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
100: 	if err != nil {
101: 		return provider.ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, Content: err.Error(), IsError: true}
102: 	}
103: 	defer func() {
104: 		_ = resp.Body.Close()
105: 	}()
106: 	statusCode = resp.StatusCode
107: 
108: 	body, err := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, maxReadBytes+1))
109: 	if err != nil {
110: 		return provider.ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, Content: err.Error(), IsError: true}
111: 	}
112: 
113: 	bodyStr := string(body)
114: 	if len(body) > maxReadBytes {
115: 		bodyStr = string(body[:maxReadBytes]) + "\n... [response truncated, exceeded 10000 characters]"
116: 	}
117: 
118: 	if resp.StatusCode < 200 || resp.StatusCode >= 300 {
119: 		return provider.ToolResult{
120: 			CallID:  call.ID,
121: 			Content: fmt.Sprintf("HTTP %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, bodyStr),
122: 			IsError: true,
123: 		}
124: 	}
125: 
126: 	return provider.ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, Content: bodyStr, IsError: false}
127: }

(End of file - total 127 lines)
</content>

parts.text:
Canonical pattern in `internal/tool` (from current implementations):

- **Entry function structure (top of file):**  
  `func <toolCamel>Entry() ToolEntry { return ToolEntry{ Definition: <toolCamel>Definition, Execute: <toolCamel>Execute } }`  
  References:  
  - `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/list_directory.go` (lines 13–19)  
  - `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/read_file.go` (lines 15–21)  
  - `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/run_command.go` (lines 23–29)

- **Definition shape (`provider.Tool`):**  
  `func <toolCamel>Definition() provider.Tool { return provider.Tool{ Name: toolname.<Const>, Description: "...", Parameters: map[string]provider.ToolParameter{ "<arg>": { Type: "string", Description: "...", Required: true|false }, ... } } }`  
  References:  
  - `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/edit_file.go` (lines 22–49)  
  - `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/url_fetch.go` (lines 48–60)  
  - `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/write_file.go` (lines 21–38)

- **Execute function placement/signature (same file, after definition):**  
  `func <toolCamel>Execute(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) (result provider.ToolResult)`  
  - Uses named return `result` to support deferred verbose logging.  
  - Returns `provider.ToolResult{ CallID: call.ID, Content: ..., IsError: ... }` on all paths (no `(result, error)` return).  
  References:  
  - `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/list_directory.go` (lines 35–90)  
  - `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/read_file.go` (lines 47–207)  
  - `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/url_fetch.go` (lines 62–127)

- **Verbose logging pattern (canonical):**  
  - Inside `Execute`, define `defer func(){ ... toolVerboseLog(ec, toolname.<Const>, result, summary) }()` near top.  
  - Build `summary` with success/error variants; include key identifier (path/url/command), and often count/size/status.  
  - Shared logger format is centralized in `toolVerboseLog`: `[tool] <toolName>: <summary> (success|error)`.  
  References:  
  - `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/verbose.go` (lines 9–22)  
  - `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/write_file.go` (lines 43–51)  
  - `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/run_command.go` (lines 50–65)  
  - `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/url_fetch.go` (lines 66–75)

- **Where new tool is wired in:**  
  Add `r.Register(toolname.<Const>, <toolCamel>Entry())` in `RegisterAll`.  
  Reference: `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/registry.go` (lines 92–99).
